task_completion.description: Generate continuous StatsD metrics to 127.0.0.1:8125 via UDP
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing a running script (e.g., bash loop, python script) or command (e.g., netcat, echo) actively sending 'payment.success:1|c' and 'payment.failure:1|c' to 127.0.0.1 port 8125.
A web browser open to http://localhost/ showing the Graphite web interface.

task_completion.description: Create 'Live Throughput' graph with success and failure rates
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph in the Graphite dashboard titled 'Live Throughput' displaying data lines for 'stats.counters.payment.success.rate' and 'stats.counters.payment.failure.rate'.

task_completion.description: Create 'Success Ratio' graph using asPercent function
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph in the Graphite dashboard titled 'Success Ratio' displaying a metric that uses the 'asPercent' function comparing success rate to the sum of all payment rates.

task_completion.description: Create 'System Impact' graph with sumSeries and secondYAxis
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph in the Graphite dashboard titled 'System Impact' displaying the 'sumSeries' of payment rates and 'servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization' plotted on a second Y-axis.
The Graphite dashboard is saved and clearly displays the title 'Payment Gateway' in the UI.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Graphite web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the agent clicking through the Graphite UI to add graphs and apply functions, rather than just editing a JSON configuration file.

integrity.description: Metrics generation script is actually running
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal shows the script executing over time (e.g., outputting logs or running in the foreground), not just a static text editor showing the script's code.

integrity.description: Graphs display actual rendered data lines
integrity.visual_evidence: The graphs in the dashboard show actual plotted lines indicating data is flowing, rather than empty graphs with spoofed titles.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is exactly 'Payment Gateway'. The background data for the 'System Impact' graph (servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization) originates from real EC2 CPU utilization data downloaded during environment setup. The exported result data is verified from /tmp/statsd_application_telemetry_result.json.
